data.title: Microservices Architecture
data.description: Instagram employs a microservices architecture to handle various functionalities like user profiles, media sharing, and messaging.
data.technical_spec: Implemented using frameworks like React Native for frontend and various microservices using Python/Django.

data.title: Content Delivery Network (CDN)
data.description: Utilizes a CDN for faster load times and efficient content delivery across global locations.
data.technical_spec: CDNs such as Akamai or Cloudflare to distribute static assets more effectively.

data.title: Load Balancing
data.description: Distributes network or application traffic across a number of servers to ensure no single server gets overwhelmed.
data.technical_spec: AWS Elastic Load Balancing or similar solutions to manage incoming traffic.

data.title: Database Sharding
data.description: User data is separated across multiple database instances to optimize retrieval and scalability.
data.technical_spec: Implemented using database solutions like PostgreSQL with sharding strategies.

data.title: GraphQL API
data.description: Provides a flexible API interface allowing clients to request exactly the data they need.
data.technical_spec: GraphQL endpoints manage interactions with the backend services.
